observations.recommendation:
Based on the foregoing, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor
create an inventory committee that will facilitate the conduct of the
physical count of its PPE as provided under COA Circular No. 2020-
006 dated January 31, 2020. In addition, to instruct the following in
relation thereto, to wit:
a. Inventory Committee to prepare the RPCPPE, in compliance
with Section 124 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I;
b. General Services Officer to maintain and update her PCs per category of
PPE, pursuant to Section 119 of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I;
c. Municipal Accountant to maintain and update her PPELC per category of
PPE, pursuant to Section 120 of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I; and
d. Ensure that the General Services Office and Municipal Accountant to
reconcile their PCs and PPELCs, respectively.

Having considered the above-cited situation, we recommended to the
Municipal Mayor to instruct the General Services Office to prepare
and submit the SSMI for CY 2022, pursuant to Section 121 of the
MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended that the Management submit to the Audit Team the
breakdown of expenditures that was charged to Other Maintenance
and Operating Expenses during the current year, pursuant to
Paragraph 106 of the IPSAS and Section 61 of PD No. 1445, otherwise
known as the “Government Auditing Code of the Philippines”.

We recommended that the concerned JO workers submit their
individual accomplishment reports duly verified and signed by
their immediate supervisors, in adherence to Section 4 (6) of PD
No. 1445, otherwise known as the “Government Auditing Code
of the Philippines” and Section 1.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001
dated June 14, 2012.. This is to ensure that the paid wages of the JO workers were
rendered in accordance with their assigned tasks and their services were truly
necessary.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the following:
a.) The Municipal Assessor to prepare and submit to the Municipal
Treasurer on or before the thirty-first (31st) day of December each
year, an assessment roll containing a list of all persons whose real
properties have been newly assessed or reassessed and the values of
such properties;
b.) The Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant a
Duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount
due and collectible for the CY; and
c.) The Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting
entries, if applicable.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to insure the following:
a. Provide necessary appropriation to facilitate the processing of
transfer of the land titles in the Municipality; and
b. Direct the General Services Officer to prioritize the titling of the
untitled lands in order to establish absolute ownership thereof.

We recommended that the Municipality procure its CSEs from the PS-
DBM. If the BAC of the Municipality desires to procure its CSE other
than the said depot on the grounds of efficiency, practicality, economic
viability and/or non-availability of stocks in such depot, the procuring
entity must secure first a CNAS from such depot.

We recommend to the Municipal Mayor to insure the following:
5.) Provide necessary appropriation to facilitate the processing of transfer of the land titles in the Municipality; and
6.) Direct the General Services Officer to prioritize the titling of the untitled lands in order to establish absolute ownership thereof.

Therefore, we recommend that the Management shall adopt the Perpetual Inventory Method in the recording and issuance of supplies and materials and comply with the process and documentation prescribed under Sections 114 and 121 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for Local Government Units (LGUs), Volume I and International Public Sector Accounting Standard (IPSAS) 12. Also, prepare the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) that shall be used as a basis in preparing the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to record the supplies and materials issued.

We recommend that the local treasurer shall review the other available records to determine if the above-stated cash advances were already settled or not, in compliance with Item 1.2 of Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant shall ensure that transactions are well-supported with its journal entry voucher/s, pursuant to Section 31 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for Local Government Units (LGUs), Volume II.

We recommend that the Municipality should procure its common-use supplies and equipment (CSEs) from the Department of Budget and Management- Procurement Service. If the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) of the Municipality desires to procure its CSEs other than the said depot on the grounds of efficiency, practicality, economic viability and/or non-availability of stocks in such depot, the procuring entity must secure first a Certificate of Non-Availability of Stocks (CNAS) from such depot.

7.13 Therefore, we recommend that the Municipal Mayor shall create an
inventory committee that will facilitate the conduct of the physical
count of its Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) as provided under
Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31,
2020. Also, to instruct the following in relation thereto, to wit:
a. Inventory Committee shall prepare the Report on the Physical
Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE), in
compliance with Section 124 of the Manual on the New
Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for Local
Government Units (LGUs), Volume I;
b. General Services Officer shall maintain and update her
property cards per category of property, plant and equipment,
pursuant to Section 119 of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I;
c. Municipal Accountant shall maintain and update her property
ledger cards per category of property, plant and equipment,
pursuant to Section 120 of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I; and
d. Ensure that the General Services Office and Municipal
Accountant shall reconcile their property cards and property
ledger cards, respectively.

8.6 We recommend to the Municipal Mayor to insure the following:
a. Provide necessary appropriation to facilitate the processing of
transfer of the land titles in the Municipality; and
b. Direct the General Services Officer to prioritize the titling of the
untitled lands in order to establish absolute ownership thereof.

9.8 We recommend that the Management shall adopt the Perpetual
Inventory Method in the recording and issuance of supplies and
materials and comply with the process and documentation prescribed
under Sections 114 and 121 of the Manual on the New Government
Accounting System (MNGAS) for Local Government Units (LGUs),
Volume I and International Public Sector Accounting Standard
(IPSAS) 12. Also, prepare the Summary of Supplies and Materials
Issued (SSMI) that shall be used as a basis in preparing the Journal
Entry Voucher (JEV) to record the supplies and materials issued.

10.3 We recommend that the local treasurer shall review the other available
records to determine if the above-stated cash advances were already
settled or not, in compliance with Item 1.2 of Commission on Audit
(COA) Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

11.5 We recommend that the Municipal Accountant shall ensure that
transactions are well-supported with its journal entry voucher/s,
pursuant to Section 31 of the Manual on the New Government
Accounting System (MNGAS) for Local Government Units (LGUs),
Volume II.

17.5 We recommend that the Municipality should procure its common-use
supplies and equipment (CSEs) from the Department of Budget and
Management – Procurement Service. If the Bids and Awards
Committee (BAC) of the Municipality desires to procure its CSEs other
than the said depot on the grounds of efficiency, practicality,
economic viability and/or non-availability of stocks in such depot, the
procuring entity must secure first a Certificate of Non-Availability of
Stocks (CNAS) from such depot.

b. Prepare a
report thereon
to be
submitted to
the Audit
Team,
pursuant to
Section 124,
Volume I of
the New
Government
Accounting
System
(NGAS)
Manual and
the Municipal
Accountant
for
reconciliation
with that of
the
accounting
records; and
c. Make the
necessary
adjustments
thereon.
observations.reason: Appraisal of certain properties conducted by competent appraiser.

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to attach the lacking documents identified under audit to satisfy the noted deficiencies, pursuant to Section 4 (6) of the Presidential Decree (P.D.) No. 1445, otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines.

Provide or establish controls to monitor the status of the appropriation of the EMEs in order to avoid overdraft on its allotment or disbursement, pursuant to Section 325 (h) of the Republic Act (R.A.) No. 7160, otherwise known as the Local Government Code of 1991.

The Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) should furnish the copy of all contracts entered by the Management within five (5) working days upon perfection, in accordance with Sections 3.1.1 and 3.1.2 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.
Procure directly to PS-DBM for the acquisition of common-use supplies, and if the same were not available, then secure a certification for the non-availability of stocks from the said office.

The Supply Officer should be responsible in the implementati on of completing the required data in the Purchase Orders and other Accountable Forms before processing the same, pursuant to Section B of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 96-010 dated August 15, 1996.

The Municipal Accountant shall check the individual charging of the Government Equity Account for CY 2020 and a justification as to the change in accounting policy adapted by the Municipality must be provided to the Office of the Auditor for evaluation.

The Local Chief Executive (LCE) should direct the concerned personnel to perform the following; 1. perform the physical inventory of the PPE. 2. Submit the Report on the Physical Count of the Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) to the audit team pursuant to Section 124 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) For Local Government Units (LGUs), Volume I.
observations.managementAction: The Local Chief Executive will create an inventory committee to conduct the physical count of Property, Plant and Equipment of the Municipality and will immediately comply thereof.
observations.reason: There is reason at this time why we couldn’t comply with the same.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendations:

a. To conduct physical inventory of its PPE for CY 2020, pursuant to Section
156 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 92-386 dated October
20, 1992.

b. Prepare a report thereon to be submitted to the Audit Team, pursuant to
Section 124, Volume I of the New Government Accounting System (NGAS)
Manual and the Municipal Accountant for reconciliation with that of the
accounting records; and

c. Make the necessary adjustments thereon.

Recommendation
Require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to attach
the lacking documents identified under audit to satisfy the noted deficiencies
from responsible persons, pursuant to Section 4 (6) of the Presidential
Decree (P.D.) No. 1445, otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of
the Philippines

Recommendation
Provide or establish controls to monitor the status of appropriation of
the EMEs in order to avoid overdraft on its allotment or disbursement,
pursuant to Section 325 (h) of the Republic Act (R.A.) No. 7160, otherwise
known as the Local Government Code of 1991.

Recommendation
The Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC)
should furnish the copy of all contracts entered by the Management within
five (5) working days upon perfection, in accordance with Sections 3.1.1 and
3.1.2 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2009-001 dated
February 12, 2009.

Recommendation
Procure directly to PS-DBM for the acquisition of common-use
supplies, and if the same were not available, then secure a certification for the
non-availability of stocks from the said office.

Recommendation
The Supply Officer should be responsible in the implementation of
completing the required data in the Purchase Orders and other Accountable
Forms before processing the same, pursuant to Section B of the Commission
on Audit (COA) Circular No. 96-010 dated August 15, 1996.

Recommendation
The Municipal Accountant shall check the individual charging made to the
Government Equity Account for CY 2020 and a justification as to the
change in accounting policy adapted by the Municipality must be provided to
the Office of the Auditor for evaluation.

We recommend the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator/personnel of the concerned Office to furnish the Auditor upon its perfection the contracts actually entered into by the Municipality of Datu Piang, Province of Maguindanao pursuant to Sections 3.1.1 and 3.1.2 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.
observations.managementAction: Admittedly, there were partial submissions made by the management of the government contracts entered by the Municipality, however, delays have occurred. However, they have committed to comply with the said Circular in the ensuing years.
observations.reason: Due to COVID-19 crisis, the Municipality has encountered difficulties in transporting the contracts and health condition of their employees were not able to prioritize to do so amidst the pandemic.

observations.recommendation: Direct the General Service Officer to submit the Procurement Monitoring Report to the Government Procurement Policy Board (GPPB), pursuant to Section 12.2 of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of R.A. No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: Admittedly, the Procurement Monitoring Report has been submitted to other oversight offices. However, in view of your finding, we promise to strictly comply with your recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation
The Local Chief Executive (LCE) should direct the personnel concerned the
following;
1. Create an inventory committee to conduct the physical inventory of
properties in accordance with Section 156 of the Commission on
Audit (COA) Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992.
2. Submit the Report on the Physical Count of the Property, Plant and
Equipment (RPCPPE) to the audit team pursuant to Section 124 of
The Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS)
For Local Government Units (LGUs), Volume I.

We recommend the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the
Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator/personnel concerned to
furnish the audit team all the contracts entered into by the Municipality of
Datu Piang, Province of Maguindanao pursuant to Sections 3.1.1 and 3.1.2 of
the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12,
2009.

Direct the General Service Officer to submit the Procurement
Monitoring Report to the Government Procurement Policy Board (GPPB),
pursuant to Section 12.2 of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations
of the Republic Act (R.A.) No. 9184 otherwise known as the Government
Procurement Reform Act.
Instruct the Accountant to prepare and submit the Bank Reconciliation Statements every month.

We reiterated PMR and management agreed to prepare the prescribed procurement monitoring report that would represent all procurement activities for each semester and submit the same to the GPPB pursuant to Section 12.2 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184, copy furnished the Office of the Auditor.

Require the Municipal General Services Officer; a.) To complete conduct physical inventory of all its properties, prepare and submit the Report on the Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) and b.) To coordinate with the Accounting Office for the reconciliation of all records.
observations.managementAction: However, during our exit conference, the management promised to conduct physical count of their PPE after having on hand the records of the PPE as a result of their efforts to locate the same.
observations.reason: Non-availability of records on PPE prevented the management to perform physical count of PPE.

observations.recommendation: We commended the management for diligently withholding and remitting premium contributions and obligations due to other government agencies.

Werecommended and management agreed to prepare the prescribed procurement monitoring report that would represent all procurement monitoring activities for each semester and submit the same to the GPPB pursuant to Section 12.2 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184, copy furnished the Office of the Auditor.

Instruct the Accountant to prepare and submit the Bank Reconciliation Statements every month.
Request the Treasurer to retrieve all bank statements monthly and furnish the same to the Accountant for the preparation of the BRS.

We reiterated and management agreed to prepare the prescribed procurement monitoring report that would represent all procurement monitoring activities for each semester and submit the same to the GPPB pursuant to Section 12.2 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184, copy furnished the Office of the Auditor.

Require the Municipal General Services Officer; a.) To conduct physical inventory of all its properties, prepare and submit the Report on the Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) and b.) To coordinate with the Accounting Office for the reconciliation of all records.
We commended the management for diligently withholding and remitting premium contributions and obligations due to other government agencies.

Instruct the Accountant to prepare and submit the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements for CY 2017. Request the Treasurer to retrieve all bank statements for the year 2017 and furnish the same to the Accountant for the preparation of the BRS.

Require the Municipal General Services Officer to conduct physical inventory of all its properties and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) and to coordinate with the Accounting Office for the reconciliation of the records.

We strongly commended the effective management for hiring a Certified Public Accountant (CPA) to the position of the municipal accountant and as a result, the accuracy, quality and the timeliness submission and case problem that has been encountered before shall be eliminated.
We commended the management for diligently withholding and remitting premium contributions and obligations due to other government agencies.

Recommendations:
Instruct the Accountant to prepare and submit the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements for CY 2017.
Request the Treasurer to retrieve all bank statements for the year 2017 and furnish the same to the Accountant for the preparation of the BRS.
It is highly recommended that Bank Reconciliation Statements must be prepared and submitted for each bank account on a monthly basis and to trace and adjust immediately all the reconciling items.

Recommendation:
We recommended and management agreed to prepare the prescribed procurement monitoring report that would represent all procurement monitoring activities for each semester and submit the same to the GPPB pursuant to Section 12.2 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184, copy furnished the Office of the Auditor.

Recommendation:
Require the Municipal General Services Officer to conduct physical inventory of all its properties and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) and to coordinate with the Accounting Office for the reconciliation of the records.

Recommendation:
We strongly commended the management for hiring a Certified Public Accountant (CPA) to the position of the municipal accountant effective this CY 2018, in which case problem that has been encountered before shall be eliminated.

Despite our appreciation, we recommend to the concerned employees in-charge of the remittances to keep up the good work so as to avoid unnecessary penalty for delayed remittances and for the employees’ record updated.

a)	We hereby recommend that the Accountant be instructed to prepare and submit the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements for the period January to December 2016.
b)	Request the Treasurer to retrieve all bank statements for the year 2016 and furnish the same to the Accountant for the preparation of the BRS.

Require the Municipal General Services Officer to conduct physical inventory of all its properties and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) and to coordinate with the Accounting Office for the reconciliation of the records.

We strongly recommend to the management to hire a Certified Public Accountant and manned the position of the municipal accountant in which case problem that has been encountered before could have been eliminated.

a)	We hereby recommend that the Accountant be instructed to prepare and submit the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements for the period January to December 2015.

b)	Request the Treasurer to retrieve all bank statements for the year 2015 and furnish the same to the Accountant for the preparation of the BRS.

Recommendation:
Despite our appreciation, we recommend to the concerned employees in-charge of the remittances to keep up the good work so as to avoid unnecessary penalty for delayed remittances and for the employees’ record updated.

Recommendation:
Instruct the Accountant to prepare and submit the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements for CY 2016.
Request the Treasurer to retrieve all bank statements for the year 2016 and
furnish the same to the Accountant for the preparation of the BRS.

Recommendation:
Require the Municipal General Services Officer to conduct physical inventory of all its properties and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) and to coordinate with the Accounting Office for the reconciliation of the records.

Recommendation:
We strongly recommend to the management to hire a Certified Public Accountant and manned the position of the municipal accountant in which case problem that has been encountered before could have been eliminated.

It is recommended that in future collections, the Municipal Treasurer should see to it that all collections will be deposited intact daily or at least once a week, or as soon as the collections reach  P10,000.00, in order to avoid the risk of  possible loss or misuse.

•	The Municipal Accountant should reconcile the outstanding loan balance per Books and the balance per PNB-Cotabato branch, and prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries (AJE) to correct the Book balance.

•	Secure from PNB-Cotabato branch the Schedule of Loan Amortization in order to be properly guided as to the actual amount to be charged as Interest Expense and deduction from the Loans Payable account.

•	Since the payment for the loan was debited by the bank through a debit memo, and not through the issuance of check, this should be recorded directly in the General Journal and a JEV should also be prepared.

It is recommended for the agency to open a Special Trust Fund account purposely for the unexpended balance at year end and transfer to this account all the unexpended balance of LDRRM Funds as of December 31, 2015.

•	Observe strictly the due dates for the remittance to BIR, GSIS, PHIC, HDMF in order to update the records of the employees and to avoid any surcharges or penalties that might be imposed due to the delay.

•	Furnish the Audit Team with the Official Receipts of the abovementioned remittances without the corresponding OR.

•	Observe strictly the correct journal entries to be used in recording the disbursement for payroll and the remittance of GSIS, PAG-IBIG and PHILHEALTH contributions, always taking into consideration the amounts to be recognized as expense or not, in order to reflect a more accurate and reliable financial statements.

•	Upon payment of the cash advance for payroll, the Due to BIR, Due to GSIS, Due to Pag-IBIG and Due to Philhealth should be used for the amounts deducted from the salaries of employees representing their personal contributions and loan payment, which are to be remitted to the concerned NGAs/GOCCs.

•	The gross amount of the salaries should be recognized as expense to the Salaries & Wages expense account.

Make the necessary adjusting entries to correct the above transactions. And henceforth, the Accountant should strictly observe the correct journal entries to be made in recording the transactions. Always refer to the NGAS manual for the proposed entries.

The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer should be instructed to submit immediately to the Audit Team the Disbursements Vouchers and RCI of the Trust and Special Educational Funds for the CY 2015.

We recommend for the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to verify if the above mentioned checks were cancelled or not. If these were cancelled, submit to the Audit Team the original cancelled checks. If these were not cancelled, submit the DVs for which these checks were issued.

Require the Municipal General Services Officer to conduct physical inventory of all its properties and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) and to coordinate with the Accounting Office for the reconciliation of the records.
